text: The surgical setup includes a trocar configuration for the SI robot during a low anterior resection, featuring two twelve millimeter ports on the right side, two eight millimeter ports on the left side, and a five millimeter port positioned between the two twelve millimeter ports on the right.

The surgeon uses sharp and blunt dissection to separate the small bowel from the retroperitoneum. After mobilizing the small bowel, it is positioned in the right upper quadrant. The focus then shifts to addressing adhesions between the sigmoid colon and the bladder.

The surgeon completes the division of adhesions between the sigmoid colon and bladder, gaining access to the pelvis at the peritoneal reflection. The sigmoid colon is straightened, and the mesentery is tensioned appropriately. A medial to lateral dissection is initiated in the TME plane using sharp and blunt techniques, with minimal bleeding indicating the correct dissection plane. Unable to locate the ureter medially, the surgeon shifts focus to the lateral aspect of the bowel, taking down lateral adhesions until meeting the medial dissection plane.

The surgeon identifies the ureter and protects it before focusing on the mesentery of the colon. Using a vessel sealer, the inferior mesenteric artery is ligated, and the mesentery is dissected at a level free of gross disease. The proximal bowel appears soft, and the surrounding fat is cleared to ensure hemostasis during the stapling of the proximal portion of the specimen.

The surgeon focuses on the distal portion of the specimen at the rectosigmoid junction, identifying the rectum and dissecting the mesentery at the chosen level. Cephalad retraction by the robotic third arm maintains tension for exposure. A robotic stapler with a green load is used to transect the bowel, suitable for thicker tissue.

The surgeon places an anvil of an EA stapler through the right lower quadrant twelve millimeter port into the peritoneal cavity, with an O-silk suture attached to the anvil's plastic head. A colotomy is made through the specimen, extending due to the thickened bowel wall from disease, allowing the anvil to pass proximally. The proximal portion of the specimen is transected with a robotic stapler and extracted through the same port, using a gel port mini as a wound protector. The suture is pulled through the proximal bowel with a needle driver, threading the anvil end through the staple line, and a clip-up layer is used to position the anvil correctly.

The surgeon inserts the EEA stapler into the end of the anvil held by the robotic arm, closes and fires the stapler, and then extracts it from the anus. An air leak test is performed, showing no bubbles upon insufflation.
